description_long: This is a bulbous, earthenware vase with a rounded body and a narrow, cylindrical neck that flares slightly at the rim. The surface is matte and displays a natural, unglazed terracotta color with subtle variations and patches of lighter and darker tones. A single, thick handle is attached to the upper body, curving from the neck to the shoulder, and the front features a stylized human face in relief, complete with prominent eyes, nose, and mouth, as well as two raised, rounded protrusions resembling hands or decorative elements. The overall form is rustic and hand-crafted, with visible irregularities and a tactile, rough finish. The vase appears hollow and functional, suitable for holding water or flowers, and its anthropomorphic design suggests a decorative or ceremonial purpose. The object is well-preserved, with no visible cracks or major damage, and would be a striking accent in a public or private urban setting.
description: A rounded terracotta vase with a single handle and a stylized human face in relief on the front, featuring a rustic, matte finish and earthy tones.
description_view_0: Side view showing the handle and the profile of the raised face and decorative protrusions.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the stylized human face and two rounded protrusions on the body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the handle visible and the face in profile, showing the smooth, rounded body.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the handle attachment and the unadorned back of the vase.
